user_outcome: `sworn regress` runs the Go test suite from the directory that contains go.mod, not the worktree root — so a repo whose Go module lives in a subdirectory (e.g. fired's `<repo>/go`) reports real regression results instead of a spurious `go test ./...: directory prefix . does not contain main module` setup failure.
rationale: Capture-brief Defect 1, surfaced downstream during fired's /merge-track T3-tax-constants (sworn 0.1.0 @ v0.6.3). internal/gate/regress.go runGoSuite (regress.go:120-121) invokes runner.Run(worktree, "go", "test", "./...") with cmd.Dir set to the worktree ROOT. sworn's own module is at the repo root so its regress passes; fired's module is at <repo>/go, so `go test ./...` from the root errors with exit 1 and the sweep reports a phantom Go FAIL on a clean, verified track. The tell it's a tool bug not a regression: the identical failure reproduces on the pristine base branch. Fix: discover the Go module directory under the worktree (nearest go.mod) and run `go test ./...` with cmd.Dir = that module dir. Do NOT run `go test ./go/...` from the root — the module root must be the CWD.
in_scope: internal/gate/regress.go runGoSuite: discover the Go module directory under the worktree (locate the go.mod nearest the worktree root — repo-root go.mod, else a single-level subdir like <worktree>/go) and set cmd.Dir to that directory when invoking `go test ./...`
A skip/degrade path when no go.mod is found under the worktree (report the Go suite as not-applicable rather than a hard FAIL), so a non-Go project's regress is unaffected
internal/gate/regress_test.go: a fixture with a Go module in a subdirectory asserting the Go suite runs from the module dir and PASSES (the fired-shaped case), plus the repo-root case still passing
out_of_scope: The TypeScript suite and golden-fixture runners (unchanged; only the Go module cwd is fixed)
Multi-module repos with more than one go.mod (single module per repo assumed; if a repo ever has several, that is a separate slice)
Any board oracle / state-resolution change (S11 and Defects 2/3 own those; this slice is regress-only and touchpoint-disjoint)

effort_complexity.rationale: One function's cwd derivation plus a subdir-module test fixture; known shape, one file of production code and its test.

risks.description: Naive module discovery could pick the wrong go.mod in a repo with nested modules or vendored go.mod files.
risks.mitigation: Scope to a single module per repo (out_of_scope names multi-module as separate); prefer the repo-root go.mod, else the shallowest single subdir go.mod, and ignore vendor/ paths.

acceptance_criteria.text: When `sworn regress` runs the Go suite against a worktree whose go.mod is in a subdirectory (e.g. <worktree>/go), runGoSuite SHALL set cmd.Dir to that module directory and `go test ./...` SHALL execute there; the Go suite result reflects the actual test outcome, not a `directory prefix . does not contain main module` setup failure. (N-14)

internal/gate/regress_test.go SHALL include a fixture with a Go module in a subdirectory asserting the Go suite runs from the module dir and passes, AND the existing repo-root-module case SHALL continue to pass; `go test ./internal/gate/...` SHALL pass. (N-14)
WHERE no go.mod exists anywhere under the worktree, the Go suite SHALL be reported as not-applicable (skipped), not a hard FAIL — a non-Go project's regress is unaffected. (N-14)
